name: Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto y Tórtoles — circular desde Piedrahita
description: This difficult 39.8-mile mountain bike route from Piedrahita explores Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto, and Tórtoles with 5265 fee
region_intro: Mountain biking around Piedrahíta offers diverse landscapes within the Corneja Valley and proximity to the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. The region features varied terrain, including forest paths, pastures, and rocky sections. Riders encounter significant elevation changes and mountain passes, providing challenging climbs and flowing descents. This area is characterized by rolling hills and dense woodlands, suitable for various mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Piedrahíta The most popular mountain bike trail is Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto y Tórtoles — circular desde Piedrahita, a difficult 39.8 miles (64.1 km) trail that takes 6 hours 1 minute to complete. This route explores the surrounding villages and features substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Peña Negra Pass loop from Piedrahíta, a difficult 23.3 miles (37.5 km) path. This trail includes the Peña Negra Pass, offering rewarding views and challenging ascents. Local mountain bikers also love the Castle of Bonilla de la Sierra loop from Piedrahíta, a 21.5 miles (34.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with moderate climbs, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Mountain biking around Piedrahíta is defined by the Corneja Valley, the Sierra de Gredos, and numerous mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Piedrahíta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 80 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Piedrahíta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Piedrahíta?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Piedrahíta, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 3 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 11 difficult options to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Piedrahíta?
A: Mountain biking in Piedrahíta offers a diverse range of terrain, from the scenic Corneja Valley to the challenging Sierra de Gredos. Expect a mix of forest paths, open pastures, and rocky sections. Routes often feature rolling hills, dense woodlands, and significant elevation changes, providing both challenging climbs and flowing descents.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Piedrahíta?
A: Yes, Piedrahíta offers 3 easy mountain bike routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Piedrahíta?
A: Experienced riders will find 11 difficult mountain bike routes around Piedrahíta. For a significant challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087786972/navacepedilla-de-corneja-casas-del-puerto-y-tortoles-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto y Tórtoles — circular desde Piedrahita&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 64 km long with over 1600m of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Villatoro – Source of the Adaja River loop from Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;, covering 57.7 km with 1150m of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer spectacular views and pass by notable landmarks. You can ride through the Corneja Valley and explore parts of the Sierra de Gredos. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Pass loop from Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;, take you over the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5216726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Pass&lt;/a&gt; itself, offering rewarding vistas. Other trails may lead you past historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4448740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Bonilla de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Piedrahíta are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087786972/navacepedilla-de-corneja-casas-del-puerto-y-tortoles-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto y Tórtoles — circular desde Piedrahita&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31459244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Bonilla de la Sierra loop from Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Piedrahíta?
A: The mountain bike trails around Piedrahíta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from the scenic Corneja Valley to the challenging ascents in the Sierra de Gredos, and the rewarding views from mountain passes like Peña Negra.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Piedrahíta?
A: Piedrahíta is suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder conditions and potential snow in the higher parts of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for families in Piedrahíta?
A: While many routes in Piedrahíta are moderate to difficult, the 3 easy routes available could be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. It's always recommended to check the specific route details for distance, elevation, and terrain to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Piedrahíta?
A: Piedrahíta serves as a common starting point for many routes, and parking is generally available within the town. For specific trailheads outside the town, it's advisable to check individual route descriptions for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots.

Q: Can I reach mountain bike trails in Piedrahíta using public transport?
A: Piedrahíta is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger towns and cities in the Ávila province. Once in Piedrahíta, many trails start directly from the town, making it possible to access them without a car. However, access to more remote trailheads might require personal transport.
tips: Rolling through the mountains of Ávila I come across this beautiful picture of the surrounding area. A delight for mountain bike lovers.

The Puente or Tío Eduardo mill is part of the ethnographic ensemble of the Corneja river mills. It is a construction of the fourteenth century, the bridge has a semicircular arch and the building collected the water from the river by means of a cacera that brought pressure to the bucket and produced light for the town of Navacepedilla de Corneja by means of a dynamo connected to the mill wheel with a pulley At that time the value of the mill was estimated at 400 reales de vellón.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike route, requiring a very good fitness level and advanced riding skills. Some sections may even necessitate pushing your bike. The technical difficulty is classified between STS S3 and S6, indicating demanding paths with potentially steep, narrow, and obstacle-ridden terrain.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is a circular trail that starts and ends in Piedrahita, connecting Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto, and Tórtoles.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Piedrahita. While specific parking details aren't provided, Piedrahita is the designated starting point, and you would typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Expect varied and demanding terrain with significant elevation changes. The route includes passes like Puerto de Peña Negra and Puerto de Chía, and you may encounter steep, narrow, and obstacle-ridden paths, potentially with treacherous ruts, requiring careful navigation and advanced bike handling.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience rich natural beauty, traversing the Corneja valley and offering views of the Castilian landscape. The route is characterized by beautiful colors and views, surrounded by forests and lush pastures. You'll also pass through charming rural towns like Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto, and Tórtoles, showcasing traditional Castilian architecture.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can discover interesting spots such as Regajos Traviesos and the historical Molino del Puente or Tío Eduardo's Mill. The trail also offers scenic views of the Sierra de Gredos and the rural towns it passes through.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the [CIMA AV01] Chía and the [CIMA AV02] Piedrahita.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, given the route's demanding nature and technical difficulty (STS S3-S6), it might be challenging for dogs, especially on longer sections or if they are not accustomed to such terrain. Always ensure your dog is fit enough and keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required for mountain biking this specific route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route would generally be during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot in this region, and winter may bring challenging conditions, including snow and ice at higher elevations, making the already difficult trail even more demanding.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several rural towns such as Piedrahita, Navacepedilla de Corneja, Casas del Puerto, and Tórtoles. These villages typically offer local amenities like cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options for visitors.
